message: Update beads for xf-16 and xf-28

 {"id":"xf-25","title":"Implement relative date formatting","description":"## Task

Create a helper function for human-friendly relative date formatting and use it throughout the codebase.

## Current State

Dates show as: '2024-01-15 10:30' or '2024-01-15 10:30:00 UTC'
Not user-friendly.

## Target Format

- < 1 minute: 'just now'
- < 1 hour: '23 minutes ago'
- < 24 hours: '3 hours ago'
- < 7 days: '3 days ago'
- < 1 year: 'Jan 15' (current year implied)
- >= 1 year: 'Jan 15, 2023'

## Implementation

Create helper function:
fn format_relative_date(dt: DateTime<Utc>) -> String {
    let now = Utc::now();
    let diff = now.signed_duration_since(dt);
    
    if diff.num_minutes() < 1 {
        return "just now".to_string();
    }
    if diff.num_hours() < 1 {
        return format!("{} minutes ago", diff.num_minutes());
    }
    // ... etc
}

## Files to Modify

- Create or add to src/format.rs
- src/main.rs: Use in print_result()
- src/repl.rs: Use in output
- src/stats_analytics.rs: Date ranges

## Testing

- Test with dates at various ages
- Test edge cases (exactly 1 hour, exactly 1 day, etc.)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Dates display in relative format in text output
- ISO format preserved for JSON/CSV output
- All edge cases handled correctly","notes":"Relative date formatting is now applied across text outputs (search results, list, tweet/thread views, DM context, stats, REPL list/show). JSON/CSV outputs remain ISO. Unit tests cover threshold cases; e2e coverage exercised via cli_e2e tests with --nocapture.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:51:06.462119-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T11:33:26.825610556-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T11:33:26.825610556-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-25","depends_on_id":"xf-19","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"xf-26","title":"Add thousands separators to large numbers","description":"## Task

Format large numbers with thousands separators for readability.

## Current State

Numbers display as: 12345678
Hard to read at a glance.

## Target State

Numbers display as: 12,345,678
Easy to read.

## Implementation

Create helper function:
fn format_number(n: i64) -> String {
    let s = n.abs().to_string();
    let mut result = String::new();
    for (i, c) in s.chars().rev().enumerate() {
        if i > 0 && i % 3 == 0 {
            result.push(',');
        }
        result.push(c);
    }
    if n < 0 {
        result.push('-');
    }
    result.chars().rev().collect()
}

Alternative: Use a crate like 'thousands' or 'num-format'.

## Files to Modify

- Create or add to src/format.rs
- src/main.rs: Use throughout
- src/stats_analytics.rs: All count displays
- src/repl.rs: Result counts

## Testing

- Test with small numbers (no separators needed)
- Test with exactly 1000, 1000000
- Test with negative numbers
- Test with zero

## Acceptance Criteria

- Numbers >= 1000 have commas
- Works correctly for all edge cases","notes":"Thousands separators now applied via shared format_number helpers across index progress, search/list outputs, stats analytics, REPL counts, and storage table stats. Added unit tests for number formatting and related helpers; cli_e2e and integration tests run with --nocapture.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:51:17.835971-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T11:34:06.58676383-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T11:34:06.58676383-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-26","depends_on_id":"xf-19","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"xf-27","title":"Improve empty results message with suggestions","description":"## Task

When search returns no results, provide helpful suggestions instead of a plain 'No results found.'

## Current State

'No results found.'
Not helpful - user doesn't know what to try next.

## Target State

No results for "xyzzy"

  Try:
    • Using different keywords
    • Checking your spelling
    • Removing date filters
    • Searching a different data type: xf search "xyzzy" --types dm

## Implementation

1. Detect empty results in cmd_search()
2. Analyze the query to provide context-aware suggestions:
   - If using filters, suggest removing them
   - If query is complex, suggest simplifying
   - If using --types, suggest trying other types

## Files to Modify

- src/main.rs: cmd_search() empty result handling
- src/repl.rs: run_search() empty result handling

## Acceptance Criteria

- Empty results show helpful suggestions
- Suggestions are context-aware when possible","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:51:29.269041-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:09:13.104945-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T10:09:13.104945-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented: hide scores, add timing, improve empty results message","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-27","depends_on_id":"xf-14","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
-{"id":"xf-28","title":"Remove unsearchable types from --types values","description":"## Task

Remove non-searchable types (follower, following, block, mute) from the --types possible values in search command.

## Current State

--types shows: tweet, like, dm, grok, follower, following, block, mute, all

But follower, following, block, mute have no text content to search. Including them confuses users and returns empty results.

## Target State

--types shows: tweet, like, dm, grok

The 'all' option should either be removed or documented to only search types with content.

## Implementation

1. In src/cli.rs, modify the SearchableType or DataType enum
2. Either create a separate SearchableType enum or filter DataType in search context
3. Update the possible_values for --types arg

## Files to Modify

- src/cli.rs: Modify possible_values or create SearchableType

## Consideration

Decide what to do with 'all':
- Option A: Remove 'all' entirely
- Option B: Keep 'all' but document it only searches content types
- Option C: Create 'content' alias that means tweet+like+dm+grok

## Acceptance Criteria

- xf search --help shows only searchable types
- Using invalid types gives clear error","notes":"Consider adding a content-only alias (content=tweet+like+dm+grok) and clarify all semantics; ensure errors list valid choices. Test plan: unit tests for enum parsing and invalid values; e2e script that runs xf search --help and a failure case with an invalid type, logging command, stdout/stderr, exit code, timing, and env.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:51:41.337913-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:54:17.098807788-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-28","depends_on_id":"xf-16","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}

 {"id":"xf-29","title":"Enhance REPL startup banner with archive info","description":"## Task

Replace the plain startup message with a rich banner showing archive info and version.

## Current State

'xf interactive mode. Type 'help' for commands, 'quit' to exit.'

Plain, no context about what archive is loaded.

## Target State

╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│  xf shell v0.5.0                                             │
│                                                              │
│  Archive: @username                                          │
│  Tweets: 12,345 • DMs: 2,341 • Likes: 8,234                  │
│                                                              │
│  Type 'help' for commands, 'quit' to exit                    │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

## Implementation

1. Load archive stats from storage at REPL startup
2. Get version from env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")
3. Format the banner with box-drawing characters
4. Print before entering the REPL loop

## Files to Modify

- src/repl.rs: run() function, add banner printing
- src/storage.rs: May need method to get quick stats

## Acceptance Criteria

- Banner shows version, username, content counts
- Box drawing characters align correctly
- Graceful fallback if stats unavailable","notes":"Ensure banner adapts to terminal width and missing stats; fall back to minimal line when stats unavailable. Test plan: unit tests for banner width calculations and formatting; e2e script that runs REPL startup with a fixture archive and logs command, stdout/stderr, exit code, timing, and env.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:51:53.294341-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:54:26.284150967-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T10:53:32.718603-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented as part of xf-17. Startup banner now shows archive username, document counts, and version.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-29","depends_on_id":"xf-17","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"xf-30","title":"Add did-you-mean suggestions for typos","description":"## Task

When users mistype command names or type values, suggest the correct option using fuzzy matching.

## Current State

'Unknown command: serach' or 'Unknown type: twet'
No suggestion for what they probably meant.

## Target State

Unknown command: 'serach'

  Did you mean 'search'?
  
  Type 'help' for available commands.

## Implementation

1. Implement Levenshtein distance function or use strsim crate
2. When parsing fails, find closest match within edit distance of 2
3. Suggest the match if confidence is high enough

fn suggest_similar(input: &str, options: &[&str]) -> Option<&str> {
    options.iter()
        .filter_map(|opt| {
            let dist = strsim::levenshtein(input, opt);
            if dist <= 2 { Some((opt, dist)) } else { None }
        })
        .min_by_key(|(_, dist)| *dist)
        .map(|(opt, _)| *opt)
}

## Files to Modify

- Cargo.toml: Add strsim = "0.10" if not present
- src/repl.rs: parse_command() error handling
- src/cli.rs or main.rs: Type parsing errors

## Acceptance Criteria

- Typos within 2 edits suggest correct spelling
- Only suggests if there's a clear match
- Works for commands, types, and targets","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:52:06.676356-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:43:55.792301-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T10:43:55.792301-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Levenshtein distance in error.rs and integrated did-you-mean suggestions for --fields and config keys. Used native implementation (no strsim dependency).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-30","depends_on_id":"xf-15","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"xf-31","title":"Fix CSV export missing carriage return handling","description":"## Bug

The CSV export in repl.rs replaces \n with space but leaves \r intact, which can cause issues in some spreadsheet software.

## Current Code (repl.rs:887)

let text_escaped = r.text.replace('"', """").replace('\n', " ");

## Fix

let text_escaped = r.text.replace('"', """").replace('\n', " ").replace('\r', "");

Or better:
let text_escaped = r.text.replace('"', """").replace(['\n', '\r'], " ");

## Files to Modify

- src/repl.rs: run_export() function
- src/main.rs: CSV output sections (if any)

## Testing

- Export data with Windows-style line endings (\r\n)
- Verify CSV opens correctly in Excel and other spreadsheet software

## Acceptance Criteria

- No \r characters in CSV output
- CSV is valid RFC 4180 format","notes":"CSV escaping now uses shared csv_escape_text across search output, export (serde-driven CSV), and REPL export; CR/LF sanitized and quotes escaped consistently. Unit tests cover CR/LF/quote handling; cli_e2e tests exercised with --nocapture.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:52:19.047685-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T11:33:46.822693307-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T11:33:46.822693307-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-31","depends_on_id":"xf-19","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
